machine.name: D3D v18.10
machine.description: Full six-side D3D frame layup package. Authors all six 14 inch square frame sides from 24 mild-steel flat bars and verifies each panel for squareness before final cube joining.
learningObjectives: Recognize the stock dimensions used to fabricate the D3D frame sides
Place all 24 flat bars in their real 14 inch square side geometry
Preserve the 1 inch overlap that converts 13 inch bar stock into a 14 inch outer square
Check each side for squareness before full frame joining
prerequisiteNotes: The six panel work zones are a tighter raised teaching layout so small real-size stock remains readable and selectable while preserving exact panel geometry.
This package covers side layup and squareness checks before final cube joining.

sourceReferences.title: OSE D3D
sourceReferences.notes: Primary machine page for the OSE 3D printer platform.

sourceReferences.title: OSE 3D Printer Manual
sourceReferences.notes: Manual and build documentation for D3D workshop builds.

sourceReferences.title: OSE Frame Construction Set
sourceReferences.notes: States that 1/8 inch by 1 inch flats are cut to 13 inch lengths and overlap to create a 14 inch square frame side.

assemblies.name: D3D Frame
assemblies.description: Complete set of six D3D frame sides authored as source-backed layup panels.
assemblies.learningFocus: Repeatable frame-side geometry, overlap logic, and squareness across the full D3D frame.

subassemblies.name: Bottom Frame Side
subassemblies.description: Lays out the bottom frame side as one 14 inch square panel.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Bottom Frame Side laid out and checked for square.

partTemplates.material: Mild steel flat bar, hot-rolled mill finish
partTemplates.function: Forms one edge of a D3D frame side.
partTemplates.defaultOrientationHint: Lay flat with the 1 inch face horizontal.

tools.name: Framing Square
tools.purpose: Confirms that each D3D frame side corner is square before final joining.
tools.usageNotes: Seat both legs of the square against adjacent flat bars.
tools.defaultOrientationHint: Hold the inside corner of the square against the bar intersection.

steps.name: Place all four bottom frame side bars
steps.instructionText: Place all four bars of the bottom frame side into their target positions. Keep each bar flat and preserve the 1 inch overlap that produces the 14 inch outer square.
steps.whyItMattersText: All four edges of the bottom frame side must stay true to the 14 inch square geometry or the full D3D frame will drift out of square.

steps.name: Verify the bottom frame side is square
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the upper-left corner marker on the bottom frame side before final joining.
steps.whyItMattersText: A square bottom frame side prevents twist and keeps the D3D frame geometry consistent across all six sides.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side checked for square.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and use it on the pulsing corner marker.
